background: The time to failure of a component follows a gamma distribution with parameters \( \alpha = 3 \) and \( \beta = 5 \). The problem involves determining the reliability of the component and the hazard rate at 10 time-units, as well as understanding the properties of the gamma distribution.

task.query: Determine the reliability of the component at 10 time-units.
Determine the hazard rate of the component at 10 time-units.
task.answer: The hazard rate is given by $$ h(10)=\frac{f(10)}{R(10)}=\frac{0.054}{0.6767}=0.798 \ \text{failures/unit time} $$

task.query: Describe the other form of the gamma probability density function (pdf) and its parameters.
task.answer: The other form of the gamma probability density function can be written as follows: $$ f(t)=\frac{\beta^\alpha t^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-t \beta} \quad t>0 $$ This pdf is characterized by two parameters: shape parameter \( \alpha \) and scale parameter \( \beta \).

task.query: Explain how the failure rate behaves depending on the value of \( \alpha \).
task.answer: When \( 0<\alpha<1 \), the failure rate monotonically decreases; when \( \alpha>1 \), the failure rate monotonically increases; when \( \alpha=1 \), the failure rate is constant.

task.query: Provide the mean, variance, and reliability of the gamma random variable.
task.answer: The mean, variance, and reliability of the gamma random variable are, respectively: $$ \begin{gathered} \text{Mean (MTTF)} \quad E(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta} \\ \text{Variance} \quad V(T)=\frac{\alpha}{\beta^2} \\ \text{Reliability} \quad R(t)=\int_t^{\infty} \frac{\beta^\alpha x^{\alpha-1}}{\Gamma(\alpha)} e^{-x \beta} d x \end{gathered} $$

task.query: Provide the coefficient of skewness for the gamma distribution.
task.answer: The coefficient of skewness of this gamma distribution is given by $$ S_c=\frac{2}{\sqrt{\alpha}} $$

meta_info.chapter: Chapter 2: Distribution Functions and Its Applications
